steps.title: Identify the introductory position
steps.content: The blank begins an introductory phrase before the main clause “her painting can now be found...” This positions the verb form at the start of the sentence, before the comma.

steps.title: Apply the participle rule
steps.content:
In Standard English, an introductory phrase must begin with a participle, not a finite verb. “Completed” is a past participle, the correct form for this position.

The other three are finite verbs unsuitable for introductory phrases; additionally, “will complete” and “completes” contradict the past date “1557.” That matches choice B — B is correct.
